user_message: I need you to process this exec meeting notes from Lucerne Publishing. We had a deep-dive last month -- need the follow-up.

input_artifacts.content:
Meeting: whitespace analysis Discussion - Lucerne Publishing
Date: 2024-01-04
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Laura Anderson (Lucerne Publishing), Tom Rodriguez (Microsoft), Priya (Lucerne Publishing)

[00:02:00] Laura Anderson (Lucerne Publishing): Good morning. I wanted to follow up on whitespace analysis from our last session.

[00:07:00] Priya (Lucerne Publishing): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is stakeholder alignment.

[00:12:00] Priya (Lucerne Publishing): Our director reviewed this and has concerns about stakeholder alignment. Can we address those?

[00:17:00] Laura Anderson (Lucerne Publishing): The timeline for security compliance is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:19:00] Tom Rodriguez (Microsoft): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is resource allocation.

[00:24:00] Tom Rodriguez (Microsoft): The timeline for technical validation is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:28:00] Priya (Lucerne Publishing): Our architect reviewed this and has concerns about technical validation. Can we address those?

handoffs.condition: ECIF workscope or partner engagement warranted

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF49-A trap (Workscope deliverables not measurable); produces complete Workshop recap, ECIF workscope draft (if warranted), partner plan, follow-up items with owners, mile
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF49-A failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF49-A failure mode (Workscope deliverables not measurable)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct whitespace analysis decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF49-A -- Workscope deliverables not measurable
